endorsement_reason: The claim correctly identifies that with a p-value of 0.04, approximately 4% of papers would be incorrectly asserting statistical significance, which aligns with standard interpretations of p-values.

The claim correctly calculates that with a p-value of 0.04, which is below the significance threshold of 0.05, 4% of articles would incorrectly claim statistical significance. This aligns with the assumption provided.
The claim correctly interprets that if the average p-value is 0.04, then approximately 4% of the papers would be incorrectly asserting statistical significance, based on the definition of p-values.
The claim accurately states that approximately 1 out of 25 papers published by Nature in 2020 would be incorrect in their claims of statistical significance, given an average p-value of 0.04.
